info.name: Hookbase API
info.description:
Complete API collection for Hookbase - Webhook routing, transformation, and delivery platform.

Authentication: All endpoints (except Health and Webhook Ingest) require a Bearer token. Use an API key with the `whr_` prefix.

For more information, visit https://docs.hookbase.app

item.name: Sources
item.description: Webhook source management. Sources are endpoints that receive incoming webhooks.

request.description: List all webhook sources with optional filtering and pagination.

Create a new webhook source. The webhook URL will be: https://api.hookbase.app/ingest/{orgSlug}/{sourceSlug}

Providers: github, stripe, shopify, slack, twilio, custom, generic
Get source details including webhook URL, verification config, and event count.
Update source details. The slug cannot be changed after creation.
Delete a source. Also deletes all associated events and deliveries.
Rotate the signing secret for a source. The old secret is immediately invalidated. The new secret is only shown once.
Export sources as a JSON file for backup or migration. Signing secrets are redacted by default.
Import sources from a JSON export file. Conflict strategies: skip, rename, overwrite. Set validateOnly to true for a dry run.
Delete multiple sources in a single request. Also deletes all associated events and deliveries.

item.name: Destinations
item.description: Webhook destination management. Destinations are endpoints where webhooks get delivered.

request.description: List all webhook destinations with optional filtering and pagination.

Create a new webhook destination.

Auth types: none, basic, bearer, api_key, custom_header
Create a warehouse destination that archives webhooks to an S3 bucket. Requires Pro or Business plan.
Create a warehouse destination that archives webhooks to a Cloudflare R2 bucket via S3-compatible API. Requires Pro or Business plan.
Create a warehouse destination that archives webhooks to a Google Cloud Storage bucket. Requires Pro or Business plan.
Create a warehouse destination that archives webhooks to Azure Blob Storage. Requires Pro or Business plan.
Get destination details including retry policy, auth config, and delivery stats.
Update destination details. All fields are optional.
Delete a destination. Removes it from all routes. Delivery history is preserved.
Send a test webhook to verify the destination is reachable and configured correctly.
Get health metrics for a destination including success rate, latency percentiles, and status.
Export destinations as a JSON file for backup or migration. Auth credentials are redacted by default.
Import destinations from a JSON export file. Conflict strategies: skip, rename, overwrite.
Delete multiple destinations in a single request.

item.name: Routes
item.description: Webhook routing configuration. Routes connect sources to destinations with optional transforms and filters.

request.description: List all routes with optional filtering by enabled status, source, or destination.
Create a new route connecting a source to one or more destinations. Supports transforms, filters, failover, and circuit breaker configuration.
Get route details with expanded source, destinations, transform, and filter.
Update route configuration. The sourceId cannot be changed after creation.
Delete a route. Does not delete the source, destinations, transform, or filter.
Get statistics for a route over a specified time period.
Get the current circuit breaker state for a route. States: closed, open, half_open.
Manually reset the circuit breaker to closed state.
Update the circuit breaker configuration for a route.
Export routes as a portable JSON file using slugs instead of IDs.
Import routes from a JSON export file. All referenced sources, destinations, filters, and transforms must exist.
Pause or resume multiple routes at once. Set isActive to false to pause, true to resume.
Delete multiple routes at once. This action is irreversible.

item.name: Events
item.description: Webhook event management. Events are webhook payloads received by sources.

request.description: List webhook events with optional filtering by source, status, date range, and more.
Get event details including optional payload and delivery attempts.
Get the raw webhook payload as received.
Get all deliveries for a specific event.
Get detailed timing information for an event including receive, validate, queue, and delivery timestamps.
Replay an event to all or specific destinations. Omit destinationIds to replay to all.
Replay multiple events at once.
Delete an event and all associated deliveries and stored payload.

item.name: Deliveries
item.description: Webhook delivery management. Deliveries represent attempts to send webhooks to destinations.

request.description: List webhook deliveries with optional filtering by status, event, destination, route, and date range.
Get delivery details including all retry attempts.
Manually retry a failed delivery. Resets the attempt count and queues for immediate retry.
Replay multiple failed deliveries in a single request (up to 100). Only deliveries with status failed or circuit_open can be replayed.
Get delivery statistics including success rate and latency percentiles.
List messages in the dead letter queue for manual review.
Requeue a message from the dead letter queue for delivery.
Delete a message from the dead letter queue.

item.name: Transforms
item.description: Payload transformation configuration. Transforms modify webhook payloads before delivery using JSONata, JavaScript, Liquid, or XSLT.

request.description: List all transforms with optional search and type filtering.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "GitHub to Slack Formatter",
  "description": "Format GitHub push events for Slack",
  "type": "jsonata",
  "code": "{ \"text\": \"New push to \" & repository.full_name & \" by \" & pusher.name }",
  "inputFormat": "json",
  "outputFormat": "json"
}

request.description: Create a new transform. Types: jsonata, javascript, liquid, xslt. Code is validated on creation.
Get transform details including code and usage count.
Update a transform. All fields are optional.
Delete a transform. Cannot delete if assigned to active routes.

item.name: Filters
item.description: Event filtering configuration. Filters control which webhook events are forwarded through a route.

request.description: List all filters with optional search.
Create a new filter. Operators: equals, not_equals, contains, not_contains, starts_with, ends_with, matches, exists, not_exists, gt, gte, lt, lte, in, not_in. Logic: AND or OR.
Get filter details including conditions and usage count.
Update a filter. Conditions replace existing ones entirely.
Delete a filter. Cannot delete if assigned to active routes.

item.name: Schemas
item.description: JSON Schema validation. Schemas define the expected structure of webhook payloads.

request.description: List all schemas with optional search.
Create a new JSON Schema (draft-07) for webhook payload validation.
Get schema details including the full JSON Schema definition.
Update a schema. Updating the schema field auto-increments the version number.
Delete a schema. Cannot delete if assigned to active routes.

item.name: Analytics
item.description: Real-time and historical analytics for webhook events, deliveries, and source performance.

request.description: Get analytics overview including totals, source breakdown, destination metrics, and status breakdown.
Get time-bucketed event counts for charting.
Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream for real-time dashboard updates. Event types: stats, event, delivery, alert.

item.name: Tunnels
item.description: Local development tunnel management. Tunnels expose local development servers to receive webhooks.

request.description: List all tunnels for the organization.
Create a new tunnel for local development. Custom subdomains require Pro plan. Response includes tunnelUrl, wsUrl, and authToken.
Get tunnel details.
Update tunnel properties.
Permanently delete a tunnel.
Get real-time status information including connection state and client IP.
Generate a new authentication token for connecting to the tunnel via WebSocket.
Force disconnect an active tunnel connection.

item.name: Cron Jobs
item.description: Scheduled webhook triggers. Schedule recurring HTTP requests to run automatically.

request.description: List all cron jobs for the organization.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Daily Cleanup",
  "description": "Clean up old data daily at 3am ET",
  "cronExpression": "0 3 * * *",
  "timezone": "America/New_York",
  "url": "https://api.example.com/cleanup",
  "method": "POST",
  "headers": {
    "Authorization": "Bearer api-key-here",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
  },
  "payload": "{\"older_than_days\": 30}",
  "timeoutMs": 60000
}

request.description: Create a new cron job. Uses standard 5-field cron expressions: minute hour day month weekday.
Get cron job details including schedule, next/last run times, and configuration.
Update a cron job. All fields are optional.
Delete a cron job and all its execution history.
Execute a cron job immediately, regardless of its schedule.
Get execution history for a cron job.

item.name: Cron Groups
item.description: Cron group management. Groups organize cron jobs into logical collections.

request.description: List all cron groups.
Create a new cron group to organize cron jobs.
Get cron group details with its associated cron jobs.
Update a cron group name or description.
Delete a cron group. Jobs within the group are moved to Ungrouped, not deleted.
Reorder cron groups by providing group IDs in the desired order.

item.name: Custom Domains
item.description: Manage custom domains for webhook ingestion and tunnel endpoints.

request.description: List all custom domains for the organization.
Create a new custom domain. Types: source (webhook ingestion), tunnel (tunnel endpoints). Response includes DNS records to configure.
Get custom domain details including verification and SSL status.
Check DNS records and activate the domain if verification passes.
Delete a custom domain. Reverts all URLs to the default api.hookbase.app domain.

item.name: Audit Logs
item.description: Query audit logs for all actions performed within an organization.

request.description: List audit logs with optional filtering by action, entity type, user, and date range.

item.name: Notification Channels
item.description: Notification channel management. Define where alerts are sent for route failures, recoveries, and other events.

request.description: List all notification channels.
Create a new notification channel. Types: slack, teams, discord, pagerduty, email, webhook.
Get notification channel details.
Update a notification channel name, config, or enabled status.
Delete a notification channel.
Send a test notification to verify the channel is configured correctly.

item.name: API Keys
item.description: API key management for programmatic access to the Hookbase API.

request.description: List all API keys for the organization. Key values are masked.
Create a new API key. The key value is only shown once in the response. Scopes: read, write.
Permanently revoke an API key. This action cannot be undone.

item.name: Webhook Ingest
item.description: Send webhooks to Hookbase sources. No authentication required.

request.description: Send a webhook to a source endpoint. No authentication required. URL format: https://api.hookbase.app/ingest/{orgSlug}/{sourceSlug}
